product_target.statement: Unresolved without human authority selection: sources compete between (1) Moria as a reusable GPU-resident voxel-world substrate Rust crate(s) with a walkable-world validation harness only, and (2) Product One "The Walkable World" as a product-shaped demo deliverable (generated region, third-person character, dig/place proof, public demo artifact). A third, broader framing treats the full voxel-world-substrate architecture (CA, fluids tier 2+, integrity, building) as the substrate product. No human feedback resolves which product identity and current-deliverable scope bind this planning run.

documents.notes: Top-level product framing: substrate crate consumed by a separate game; walkable-world executable described as consumer/validation harness. Points at docs/seeds/ for preserved inputs. Not self-authenticating over the seed corpus.

Seed-folder index that asserts binding status for product-one-seed.md and project-boundary.md and demotes voxel-world-substrate.md to architecture reference. README binding assertions are evidence to analyze, not self-authenticating authority.

Operator clarification candidate: product is reusable substrate crate(s); game is out of repo; walkable executable is validation harness via public APIs; System/LLM/spell/gas/combat/AI/building layers out of scope. Competes with Product One's product-shaped-demo framing for product identity.

Candidate binding_target for current deliverable scope: seed world, substrate slice, player controller, performance targets, milestones. Self-describes as product-shaped demo and pins what is built first against the architecture reference. Competes with project-boundary on whether the demo or the crate is the product.

Substrate architecture design specification (storage, gen, vegetation, fluids, integrity, building, layering, build order). Product One and the seeds index treat it as a reference whose required portions are selected by the first product slice. Broader build-order items beyond Product One remain non-current scope unless human authority expands deliverable. Companion pivot doc is missing.

claims.statement: Moria is a reusable, GPU-resident voxel-world substrate intended to be consumed by the actual game as a Rust crate.
Its walkable-world executable is a separate consumer and validation harness for terrain generation, streaming, meshing, editing, collision, persistence, and performance—not a game layer.
The substrate inputs are preserved in docs/seeds/.
product-one-seed.md defines the binding substrate implementation and its walkable-world validation harness.
voxel-world-substrate.md is the substrate architecture reference. Only the portions selected by the Product One seed are required for this milestone.
project-boundary.md records the operator's binding clarification: Moria is a reusable crate consumed by a separate game, and the included executable is only a public-API validation harness.
Moria is only the voxel-world substrate. Broader game, System, LLM, spell, gas, combat, AI, and building intent is deliberately absent and out of scope.
Moria's product is the reusable voxel-world substrate, exposed as a Rust crate or a small family of tightly scoped Rust crates.
The actual game is a separate downstream consumer and is not part of this repository.

Moria may include a walkable-world executable, but that executable is only a validation harness. It must consume the substrate through the same public interfaces available to an external game rather than owning privileged or game-specific implementation paths.

This is an immediate, concrete reason to use a Cargo workspace boundary between the reusable substrate and its validation harness. The precise crate split is a technical-design decision; the consumer boundary is not optional.

Game rules and the future System, LLM, spell, gas, combat, AI, and building layers are out of scope. Compatibility seams may be designed where the substrate requirements demand them, but those layers must not be implemented here.

A generated natural world — hills, forest, river, cliffs, caves — that you can run through in third person, where everything you see is voxel truth: you can walk to any point, the terrain is continuous and smooth, and a debug key proves it's all mutable matter underneath. The demo's job is to make one claim undeniable: this is not a heightmap with props — it's a fully material world, and it looks good.
Scope: the voxel substrate, one generated region, and a character who can run around in it. No System, no spells, no gas, no game. This is the tech proven as a product-shaped demo.
Everything here references voxel-world-substrate.md; this doc pins down what gets built first, what the seed world contains, and what "done" looks like.

Explicit non-goals: combat, stats, entities beyond the player, AI; The System / LLM; gas, pricing, intent; building UI, blueprints, mechanisms; fluids beyond static bodies (tier 1 only); weather sim, seasons, growth; persistence beyond reload same seed + deltas (single save slot, no versioning).
Dig and place stay in scope, on a debug key, as the proof that the world is fully mutable matter rather than decorative terrain.
Seed region is 1km × 1km × 256m with 25cm voxels and 16³ bricks; the whole region must not fit in memory as raw voxels.

Product one builds generation layer full; matter layer partial (brick pool, meshing, grass/clutter, voxel objects without felling, static water tier 1; no CA/fire/fluids-tier-2/3/integrity/granular settle); script/API layer sliver (dig/place verbs and mirror queries as engine-internal API only).
Third-person character controller with run, sprint, jump, swim; collision against voxel truth not render mesh; free-orbit camera; debug dig/place and visualization keys.

Performance targets include 60fps at 1440p on mid GPU / M4 Mac Mini constraints, dig-to-remesh within 2 frames, cold-start under 5s, full region under ~2GB GPU resident, delta save under 50MB; benchmarks are part of the deliverable.
Ordered demoable milestones 1–6: hill meshing, carve tunnel, true geology, dressed world, the run (controller+route), numbers (streaming/persistence/benchmarks); stretch timber felling.

Product One buys: substrate crates benchmarked with API boundary enforced; a public artifact for the X audience including a downloadable demo at milestone 5; a decision bed for open substrate questions; Product two starts from a walkable world.
This doc is a companion to system-substrate-pivot.md and details the world layer as a standalone engine substrate with zero LLM dependency.

Substrate, not game: clean layering so the same crate stack supports the System ARPG, a DF-style fortress/colony game, a Moria-style descent roguelike, or a pure sandbox. Game rules live above the substrate.
The substrate is designed as reusable foundation for multiple future games (System ARPG, fortress/colony, Moria-style descent, sandbox), not as a single finished game product.

locators.heading: 12. Layering: The Substrate as Reusable Crates

claims.statement: GPU-resident architecture per the pivot doc: brick pool, CA sim, FleX-pattern coupling (commands in, stale mirror + events out).
Voxel size for the world grid assumed at 25cm (4 voxels/meter) with 16³ bricks; open question whether 12.5cm or per-region sizing is better.

locators.heading: 2. The Look Problem: Voxels That Don't Look Like Voxels

claims.statement: Layering rules: gas is a policy object at script/API layer; the System is a game-layer client not a substrate feature; nothing above the matter layer touches voxels directly—everything goes through verbs and queries.

locators.heading: 12. Layering: The Substrate as Reusable Crates

claims.statement: Substrate vertical-slice build order includes brick pool, meshing, dig/place, geology, brick CA (fire/wetness) + tree felling, fluids tier 1+2, integrity + granular, and persistence/streaming—broader than Product One's included slice.

locators.heading: 13. Build Order (vertical slice for the substrate itself)

claims.statement: Architecture includes weather/seasons, fire ecology, structural integrity, building/blueprints/mechanisms, multi-class pathfinding, and multi-tier fluids beyond Product One non-goals; these are substrate capabilities described for multi-game reuse rather than Product One scope.
System (LLM) hooks are noted where they attach, but this substrate must stand alone as an engine layer with zero LLM dependency.

decisions.question: What is Moria's product identity for this planning run?

options.label: Reusable substrate crate(s)
options.summary: Product is the GPU-resident voxel-world substrate exposed as a Rust crate or small crate family; games are external consumers. Supported by root README, project-boundary, and seeds-index substrate-only language.

options.label: Product One walkable-world demo
options.summary: Product is Product One "The Walkable World": a product-shaped demo (generated region, character, dig proof, milestones, public/downloadable artifact). Supported by product-one-seed product statement and "what Product One buys."

options.label: Full architecture substrate
options.summary: Product is the full voxel-world-substrate design (matter CA, multi-tier fluids, integrity, building, multi-game foundation) as specified in the architecture doc build order—not only the Product One slice.

decisions.rationale: Root README and project-boundary state the product is a reusable substrate crate. Product One frames a walkable demo as the product-shaped deliverable with public artifacts and customers (future-you and X audience). The architecture doc describes a broader multi-game foundation. Seeds README asserts both a binding Product One implementation and an operator binding clarification that Moria is only the substrate—without establishing which wins when the demo-as-product and crate-as-product framings diverge. No human feedback selects among these mutually exclusive product identities.

decisions.question: What is the authority role of the walkable-world executable relative to the product?

options.label: Validation harness only
options.summary: Walkable-world executable is solely a public-API validation harness / separate consumer—not a game layer and not the product itself (root README, project-boundary, seeds README clarification).

options.label: Primary demo product
options.summary: Walkable world is the Product One deliverable itself: playable third-person demo, milestone route, downloadable public artifact (product-one-seed).

options.label: Harness that is also the demo artifact
options.summary: Same executable is both the mandatory public-API validation harness and the Product One demo artifact, without elevating game layers into the repo—requires human confirmation that both framings bind together.

decisions.rationale: Boundary and root docs insist the executable is only a validation harness. Product One describes a product-shaped playable demo and public downloadable artifact. Seeds README labels Product One as binding for implementation and harness while also recording the operator harness-only clarification. These roles can be reconciled only by human authority; analyst must not silently merge them.

decisions.question: What binds current-deliverable scope (in vs out for this milestone/planning run)?

options.label: Product One substrate slice + seed world + player
options.summary: Current deliverable is Product One's stated slice: generation full; matter partial; script/API dig-place sliver; curated 1km seed world; third-person player; milestones 1–6; architecture-doc features only when selected by Product One.

options.label: Boundary-defined crate + harness only
options.summary: Current deliverable is whatever ships a reusable substrate crate(s) plus public-API validation harness under project-boundary constraints; Product One detail is non-binding flavor unless separately selected.

options.label: Architecture vertical-slice build order
options.summary: Current deliverable follows voxel-world-substrate §13 including CA/fire, fluids tier 2, integrity/granular, and related matter features beyond Product One non-goals.

decisions.rationale: Product One and the seeds index claim Product One pins first-build scope and selects architecture portions. Project-boundary constrains out game/System/building layers without adopting Product One's demo checklist. The architecture build order is materially wider (fire CA, fluids tier 2, integrity, tree felling). Without human selection, current-deliverable scope cannot be fixed from self-assertions alone.

decisions.question: Which source documents bind product identity and current deliverable versus remaining reference or context?

options.label: Product One binding_target + project-boundary binding_constraints + VWS supporting_reference
options.summary: Adopt seeds-index hierarchy: product-one-seed.md binds implementation/harness scope; project-boundary.md binds operator product-boundary constraints; voxel-world-substrate.md is architecture reference only for selected portions; root/seeds READMEs are context/index.

options.label: Project-boundary primary product authority
options.summary: project-boundary.md is the binding product authority (crate product, harness-only executable, out-of-scope layers); Product One is supporting scope proposal; VWS is supporting architecture reference.

options.label: Product One primary product authority
options.summary: product-one-seed.md is the sole binding_target for what is built now; project-boundary and root README are supporting constraints; VWS is supporting reference.

decisions.rationale: Seeds README uses the word "binding" for both Product One and project-boundary, but filename, ordering, and in-document "binding" language are not self-authenticating. Product One and project-boundary can conflict on product identity and walkable-world role. No human feedback establishes document-role hierarchy.

deferred_design_questions.question: Final voxel size: keep 25cm, move to 12.5cm, or allow per-region sizing?

options.label: Per-region sizing (fine near settlements, coarse wilderness)

deferred_design_questions.rationale: Both Product One and the architecture doc treat 25cm as the working assumption and explicitly leave a final call to measurement. This does not change product identity or which document binds scope.

deferred_design_questions.question: Distant terrain LOD: chunked marching-cubes LOD versus imposter cards from the column index?

options.label: Chunked marching-cubes LOD for distant terrain
Imposter cards from the column index

deferred_design_questions.rationale: Listed as an open architecture question in voxel-world-substrate §14. Implementation choice after product authority is fixed.

deferred_design_questions.question: At what tree/object count does the object registry need its own spatial acceleration structure?

options.label: Defer until Product One forest counts are measured
Design object-layer spatial acceleration in the initial substrate

deferred_design_questions.rationale: Open performance/architecture question; does not determine document authority or product identity.

deferred_design_questions.question: Does fluids tier 2 need a pressure solve (DF-accurate U-bend behavior) or is momentum-only enough?
deferred_design_questions.rationale: Architecture open question; Product One excludes fluids beyond tier-1 static bodies from current scope, so this is not an authority gate for the present deliverable.

deferred_design_questions.question: What is the precise Cargo crate split inside the substrate workspace?

options.label: Small family of tightly scoped substrate crates + harness

deferred_design_questions.rationale: project-boundary states the consumer boundary is not optional but the precise crate split is a technical-design decision.

deferred_design_questions.question: Primary terrain meshing algorithm: surface nets, dual contouring, or hybrid selection rules?
deferred_design_questions.rationale: Architecture recommends dual contouring / hybrid and Product One requires GPU dirty-brick meshing, but exact algorithm choice is implementation design.

deferred_design_questions.question: Should multiplayer/server-authoritative readiness remain in scope statements even if not built now?

options.label: Keep multiplayer-ready verb/command architecture in scope statements
Omit multiplayer from scope statements until a later product

deferred_design_questions.rationale: Architecture §14 open question; does not decide current product identity.

deferred_design_questions.question: How should GPU-resident details that only the missing system-substrate-pivot.md may fully specify (FleX coupling, particle/rigid dynamic matter layer, etc.) be recovered for detailed design?

options.label: Rely on restatements inside voxel-world-substrate.md and Product One
Restore or re-author system-substrate-pivot.md before detailed GPU design

deferred_design_questions.rationale: Missing pivot is a provenance gap for architecture depth. Product identity and current deliverable are already described in present seeds; recovering pivot-only detail is a design/input problem, not an authority selection among present product targets.
